what_it_does: Documents the latent provenance spine that links every generated artefact back to its originating conversation exchange via tool_calls and exchanges, and synthesizes a design for joining the episodic-memory conversation store with the Company corpus.
observations: Provides full annotated schemas for the episodic-memory tables exchanges (18 columns) and tool_calls (7 columns).
Identifies tool_calls.tool_input as the artefact-address field, carrying file_path for Write/Edit/NotebookEdit tool calls.
Reports a verified live SQL join from tool_calls to exchanges that returns genesis session, timestamp, cwd, git_branch, and user message for written artefacts.
States that tool_calls.tool_result is 100% NULL across 6.7M rows, making it the largest dormant capability.
Notes extreme parallel activity: up to 263 concurrent sessions within a 10-minute window.
Shows that Company corpus records already carry source_address = exchange://<sid>/<i> and ts_source from the originating exchange.
Highlights that corpus.py enforces lineage (session/round/project) as a fail-loud write gate.
Reports that no Company code currently reads the episodic-memory db.sqlite, so the cross-system join is unbuilt.
Proposes a minimal buildable join using session_id and the line_start/line_end regions present on both sides.
Includes a fail-loud table of dormant/unbuilt items and risks.
standouts: The artefact-to-genesis JOIN already runs on live data today.
Company-side generated records are already anchored to exchange:// coordinates.
tool_result is completely empty despite being captured in the schema.
conventions: Uses an evidence discipline with Observed/Verified/Inferred tags.
Includes live SQL queries and row counts as supporting evidence.
Organizes content into labeled sections A/B/C/D/E with numbered subsections.
Provides an explicit dormant/unbuilt status table.
References versioned plugin cache paths and absolute filesystem locations.
concerns: The file is a synthesis/read, not executable code, so its design sections remain inferred and unbuilt.
It depends on a versioned plugin cache path that may relocate on plugin update.
The boundary-rule mismatch between Company positional index i and episodic exchange enumeration is flagged but unresolved.
Schema and row-count details may drift as the conversation store grows or migrations add columns.
notes: This read is the spine reference for the memory-merge fleet: it maps how generated artefacts, corpus records, and conversation transcripts can be joined at the exchange level, and honestly flags what is already live versus still dormant.
questions: Does the episodic-memory indexer use the same exchange-boundary rule as transcript_extract.py?
Which embedding model produces the 384-dimensional vectors in vec_exchanges?
What are the exact input schemas for the MCP tools that write Supabase rows, Obsidian notes, and Langflow graphs?
How will the join tolerate future schema drift in the exchanges table?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to establish the provenance spine all other reads hang from; the file does exactly that by documenting schema, verified joins, Company anchoring, and a synthesized merge design.
apparent_themes: Cross-system reconciliation at the conversation exchange
Evidence discipline and fail-loud honesty about built vs unbuilt
Time and parallel context as first-class dimensions
Schema drift tolerance and durability risk
intention_signals: It is trying to prove that a unified provenance graph already exists in latent form across the conversation store and Company corpus, and to define the concrete join that would make every generated artefact traceable back to the prompt that created it.
novelty: It treats the conversation transcript not merely as searchable memory but as the single genesis-ledger for all generated artefacts across filesystem, corpus, Supabase, Obsidian, and Langflow, with a bidirectional exchange-level join design.
contribution: A verified, evidence-tagged inventory of the conversation-store schema and the artefact-address mechanism, plus a neutral synthesis of how the episodic store and Company corpus already share exchange coordinates and what remains to build the join.
summary_for_embedding: A read document that maps the provenance spine connecting generated artefacts to their originating conversation exchanges through tool_calls and exchanges, and designs the cross-system join with the Company corpus.
intention_for_embedding: To establish the foundational evidence and design for re-anchoring all generated content to its transcript genesis in the memory-merge fleet.
